transcript.clean_text: 188 kilometers separated zakopane and krakov and the final start and finish towns of this 77th tour polonia remko eve nepal had almost sewn up the gc more or less with a fantastic attack 50 kilometers from the finish of stage four so it was probably down to the sprinters to finish things in krakow five breakaway riders went away originally but didn't really get too much more than a couple of minutes a couple of attackers joined them and there were seven away with 30 kilometers to go and a gap of a minute and two seconds casper kievitz joined in the end by joss van emden and of course the other man from azure desert mondial alexey gujar heading towards the final three circuit laps of krakov four kilometers each the gap was right down to 22 seconds the sprinters teams did not want to let the opportunity go it was on the second of the three laps that the breakaway ended trek segafreddo one of the teams working the hardest to pull things back as word bore a han screwer still without a stage win despite having one of the in-form sprinters in pascal ackermann as we came down to the final lap they were all together and it got very very messy lots of different sprint trains competing for position at the front of the bunch mark cavendish was there once again looking for his first win in a couple of years he though lost a foot and a pedal with one kilometer to go and would be out of the game phil bauhaus would be charged with sprinting for bahrain mclaren today it would be the turn of jasper stalvin for trek segafredo but look out for other names into the mix switch from left to right as they would from the road pascal ackermann would follow it will be starving the man to open up the sprint first belgian classics rider having a go being led out by his compatriot edward turns behind barhaus would be there but it'd be the other german ackermann to then launch his sprint and he looked all day long like he was going to win the stage but look for the man in the blue and white david ballerini who had never won a world tour race until this point hitting the front staying at the front and winning after four and a half hours in the sabbath a great end to what's been a strange week for the koenig quick step the worry on day one with the health of fabio jacobson the better news that came a couple of days later 48 hours well it's been pretty brilliant for them hasn't it renko have nepal with a performance on day four davide ballerini finishing it off here as eve nepal took the gc only the fourth win in the career of the italian and beating pascal ackermann points jersey winner at his native giro d'italia so ballerini from ackermann and dainese with gibbons and phillipson completing the top five celebrations all round after an emotional week before the kernick quickstep they win the gc at the tour of poland for the first time in their history as a team and the remco have nepal four stage races four stage race wins but this is the first time he's ever done it in a world tour race is margin of victory a big one a minute and 52 seconds over jakob full sun simon yates completed the podium
